name: Darth Talon: A Star Wars Story
description:
Darth Talon was one of the most formidable Sith of her era, a Twi'lek warrior who embodied the ruthless doctrine of the One Sith under Darth Krayt. Her rise to power was forged in the fires of pain, discipline, and unwavering loyalty to the Sith. To understand Darth Talon is to understand the brutal Sith culture that shaped her.

Early Life on Ryloth

Born on the Twi'lek homeworld of Ryloth, Talon was taken from her family at a young age, though the identity of her original clan is lost to history. Unlike many Twi'leks who were subjected to servitude, she was born into a different kind of bondage—one of darkness. Growing up, she showed an unusual sensitivity to the Force. The Sith, ever watchful for potential recruits, discovered her and took her to Korriban, the ancient home of the Sith, where she was to be trained in their ways.

On Korriban, Talon’s upbringing was stripped away. The Sith did not allow weakness, and any sign of hesitation or compassion was punished severely. She endured grueling training under the Sith Lords, learning to wield a lightsaber with deadly precision and harness the power of the dark side. She was subjected to torturous rituals meant to strengthen her connection to the dark side, her pain and anger fueling her growth.

It was here that she received the first of her Sith tattoos—markings that would come to cover her entire body, inscribed as a sign of her devotion to the Sith Code. These intricate black markings were not merely for decoration but infused with dark side energy, a symbol of her complete submission to the will of the Sith Order.

The Trial of Pain

Like all Sith apprentices, Talon had to prove her worth through brutal trials. Failure meant death. One of her most defining trials came when she was ordered to kill her own master Darth Ruyn without hesitation or mercy. This was the way of the Sith—eliminate the weak, for only the strong could rule.

Talon did not hesitate. She struck down her master in a fierce duel, demonstrating not only her skill with a lightsaber but also her absolute loyalty to the Sith. Her ability to kill without question impressed Darth Krayt, the Dark Lord of the Sith who had revived the Sith Order under his vision of the One Sith. Talon had passed her final test, and Krayt took her as one of his Hands—a personal enforcer who answered only to him.

Darth Krayt’s Hand

As one of Krayt’s Hands, Darth Talon became an instrument of his will. She executed his orders without question, hunting down enemies of the Sith and crushing any who opposed their rule. Unlike the Sith of old, who constantly schemed against one another, Talon followed Krayt with absolute loyalty, embodying the One Sith ideology that prioritized unity under a single dark ruler.

Her skills in combat became legendary. She wielded a single red lightsaber with lethal grace, using a form of combat that blended speed, agility, and aggression. Her acrobatics made her a difficult opponent to predict, and her mastery of the dark side allowed her to unleash devastating attacks, from Sith lightning to telekinetic assaults.

One of her most notable missions was hunting down and capturing Cade Skywalker, the last known heir of the Skywalker bloodline. Cade had forsaken his Jedi heritage, becoming a rogue bounty hunter, but his latent power made him a threat to Krayt’s rule. Talon engaged Cade multiple times, testing his strength and tempting him with the dark side, trying to bring him under the control of the Sith.

During their encounters, Talon attempted to seduce Cade—both physically and through the lure of power. She sensed the darkness within him and sought to turn him into an ally of the Sith. However, Cade resisted, leading to some of the most intense battles of Talon’s life.

The Fall of the One Sith

Darth Krayt’s vision of the Sith was powerful but ultimately flawed. As rebellions against the Sith
